sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: Publishing an Android app involves creating a developer account, preparing a release build, configuring store listings, declaring app content, and rolling out through structured release tracks. Google Play requires Android App Bundles (AAB), which it converts into device-optimized APKs. The Play Console manages releases, analytics, policies, and user feedback. A well-prepared release ensures smooth review and positive user experience.

sections.heading: Key Steps
items: Account Setup – Register a developer account ($25 one-time), verify identity, set profile, enable 2FA.
App & Store Listing – Set app name, description, screenshots, icon, feature graphics; declare category and contact info.
App Content – Complete content rating, privacy policy, data safety, target audience, ads, and app access.
Release Build – Increment versionCode, sign with upload key, build AAB, test locally with bundletool.
Release Tracks – Use Internal, Closed, Open Testing, then Production; staged rollouts monitor stability.
Monitoring – Track crash/ANR rates via Android Vitals; respond to reviews; maintain policy compliance.

sections.heading: Tips
items: Localize store listings for multiple markets.
Ensure Data Safety section and privacy policy are accurate and up to date.
Test releases thoroughly before promoting to wider tracks.
Use the In-App Review API to collect user ratings without disrupting the experience.
